title: Waco C-62
summary: The Waco C-62 was a proposed high wing transport airplane similar in size and capacity to the Douglas DC-3. 13 pre-production aircraft were ordered in October 1941, with a contract for 240 production models awarded in early 1942; however, the project was canceled in September 1943 in favor of the Curtiss-Wright C-76 Caravan before any aircraft were built.
text:
The Waco C-62 was a proposed high wing transport airplane similar in size and capacity to the Douglas DC-3. 13 pre-production aircraft were ordered in October 1941, with a contract for 240 production models awarded in early 1942; however, the project was canceled in September 1943 in favor of the Curtiss-Wright C-76 Caravan before any aircraft were built.

Specifications
Data from General characteristics
Crew: Two
Capacity: 22 soldiers or 4,400 pounds (2,000 kg) cargo
Length: 73 ft 10 in (22.5 m)
Wingspan: 100 ft 0 in (30.48 m)
Height: 19 ft 1 in (5.82 m)
Empty weight: 21,660 lb (9,825 kg)
Max takeoff weight: 29,500 lb (13,381 kg)
Powerplant: 2 × Pratt & Whitney R-1830-39 radial engines, 1,200 hp (890 kW)  each
Performance

Maximum speed: 150 mph (241 km/h, 130 kn)
Range: 600 mi (966 km, 522 nmi)
Service ceiling: 17,001 ft (5,182 m)
